title: fix: restore inline link styling
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Restores `InlineLink` to the amber, non-strong inline treatment.
- Removes the accidental bold/white shared link styling from PR #11943.
- Leaves the existing Enter consumers on the shared primitive.

Validation:
- `biome check --write packages/ui/src/primitives/InlineLink.tsx`
- `tsc --noEmit -p packages/ui/tsconfig.json`

gist.headline: Amber is the color of our links
gist.blurb: We accidentally painted our inline links bold and white, but we've brought back the classic amber glow you know and love.
gist.summary: Restores the amber, normal-weight styling for the InlineLink UI primitive, reverting an accidental bold/white change from a previous update.
gist.impact: Inline links across the UI will once again appear in their familiar amber color instead of bold white.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee as a painter, restoring the amber color to UI elements. The robot companion assisting with the paint.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny painter's beret, using a small brush to paint a glowing amber text link on a retro computer screen. A chunky pixel robot companion holds a bucket of glowing amber paint. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm cream gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, chunky sprites.
